DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT BRUCE MILLER'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFFS BRIAN, MARCIN, SHARRI MARCIN, AND MARION MARCIN AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS SHALL APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION AT A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE TIME AND LOCATION WITHIN FOUR WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO APPEAR FOR THEIR DEPOSITIONS WITHIN FOUR WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER, SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT MILLER AND AGAINST PLAINTIFFS (SANCTIONS ARE PAYABLE WITHIN FIVE WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CCMH FISHERMAN'S WHARF LLC, HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, INC., HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, L.P., AND BARCELO CRESTLINE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS MARION, BRIAN AND SHARRI MARCIN IS: A) GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE WORDS "AND PHYSICAL INJURIES, ALL OF" AND "PHYSICAL AND" IN LINES 22 AND 23 ON PAGE 7; B) GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD A PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE EVENT THAT THE MARCINS OBTAIN FACTS SUPPORTING PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANTS; AND C) DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. (SEE MINI MINUTES AND SCANNED ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) FORMAL ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, MARIA.TORREANOM@GMAIL.COM (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Brian, Marcin, Sharri Marcin, And Marion Marcin And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Grayson W. (Trey) Marshall III, esq. (415-273-7442) of Allen Matkins for Defendant BRUCE MILLER.
No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRUCE MILLER's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Brian, Marcin, Sharri Marcin, And Marion Marcin And Request For Sanctions.

Defendant Bruce William Miller's Motion to Compel the depositions of Plaintiffs Brian Marcin, Sharri Marcin, and Marion Marcin is GRANTED. Plaintiffs shall appear for deposition at a mutually agreeable time and location within four weeks of notice of this order. If plaintiffs fail to appear for their depositions within four weeks of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $1,500 are awarded in favor of Defendant Miller and against plaintiffs (sanctions are payable within five weeks of notice of this order).

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants CCMH Fisherman's Wharf LLC, Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc., Host Hotels & Resorts, L.P., and Barcelo Crestline Corporation's motion to strike portions of the complaint filed by plaintiffs Marion, Brian and Sharri Marcin is: a) granted without leave to amend as to the words "and physical injuries, all of" and "physical and" in lines 22 and 23 on page 7; b) granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages but without prejudice to seeking leave to amend to add a prayer for punitive damages in the event that the Marcins obtain facts supporting punitive damages liability against the moving defendants; and c) denied as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. The Marcins agree to delete the words "and physical injuries, all of" and "physical and" in lines 22 and 23 on page 7. The complaint does not allege sufficient ultimate facts showing that any of the moving defendants acted despicably. Penner v. Falk (1984) 153 Cal. App. 3d 858 does not support the sufficiency of the Marcins' prayer for punitive damages. Unlike Penner, the Marcins have not alleged any facts showing long-existing conditions portending danger that were ignored by the moving defendants. Perhaps even more significantly, subsequent to Penner Civil Code 3294 was amended to make it harder to obtain punitive damages by, among other things, requiring that a defendant engage in despicable conduct for punitive damages based on malice and oppression. None of the other words sought to be stricken are so obviously irrelevant or abusive that they should be removed from the complaint. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, maria.torreanom@gmail.com (302/HK)
